LAW AND MOTION 302, DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT CONTINUED FROM OCT-15-2015 TO LAW AND MOTION ON OCT-28-2015 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS GERARD BUULONG AND ALI KESHAVARZ' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO OCTOBER 28, 2015 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPT. 302, LAW & MOTION, TO GIVE PLAINTIFF MARIA RODRIGUEZ ET AL. THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. PLAINTIFF MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN OCTOBER 20, 2015 AT 4:00 PM. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: SEAN KANE, NOT REPORTED. (302/EHG)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFFS STATE THAT THEY E-FILED THEIR AMENDED COMPLAINT ON FEBRUARY 2, 2015, THE AMENDED COMPLAINT DOES NOT APPEAR IN THE COURT'S REGISTER OF ACTIONS. PLAINTIFFS SHALL FILE THEIR AMENDED COMPLAINT WITHIN 20 DAYS OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER FOR SIGNATURE. JUDGE: ERNEST H. Off Calendar Nov-13-2015 Continued To Feb-10-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-28-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS GERARD BUULONG AND ALI KESHAVARZ'S SPECIAL AND GENERAL DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained in part and denied in part. Defendants' demurrer is sustained, with ten days leave to amend, to allow Plaintiffs an opportunity to allege facts that show how any of the named defendants are responsible for the negligent design and manufacturing of the vehicle involved in the accident. Defendants' demurrer, on the grounds that Plaintiffs impermissibly added Defendant Luc Phung and Peasant Pies, Inc. to the complain, is denied because Defendant Luc Phung and Peasant Pies, Inc. were properly added to the complaint under the relation back theory. (CCP section 474; See McOwen v. Grossman (2007) 63 Cal.Rptr.3d 615.) Defendants do not present any evidence to suggest that Plaintiffs knew of Defendant Peasant Pies, Inc. and Defendant Phung's identity or involvement in the matter at the time that they filed the original complaint. The way in which Plaintiffs list the bases for the cause of action does not preclude Plaintiffs from adding Defendant Luc Phung and Defendant Peasant Pies, Inc. to the complaint, nor does it require the court to sustain Defendants' demurrer. (See Marasco v. Wadsworth (1978) 21 Cal.3d 82, 86, 88.). Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-26-2015 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS GERARD BUULONG AND ALI KESHAVARZ'S SPECIAL AND GENERAL DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT FOR NONJOINDER OF PARTIES; FAILURE TO STATE FACTS SUFFICIENT TO CONSTITUTE A CAUSE OF ACTION; AND UNCERTAINTY is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. Plaintiffs shall file their amended complaint within 20 days of entry of this order. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER FOR SIGNATURE. Judge: Ernest H.
